variants: Flavien Bouyx is a prominent figure in the Civil engineering field, where he has established his expertise through years of dedicated study and research. He completed his doctoral work under the guidance of mentor Kelvin Dlamini, who played a pivotal role in shaping his academic career. Beyond his engineering pursuits, Flavien is also associated with the Moonbay Driftwood Artisans, where he shares his passion for craftsmanship. His innovative work has been recognized with support from the Curio Pathmakers Council, which serves as his primary funding source. Additionally, he holds the Fractal Climatologist Badge, showcasing his specialized knowledge in climate-related engineering challenges.
